channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Texas Democrat says Trump's tariffs 'will definitely get Mexico to the table' to solve immigration, fentanyl problems
item.description: Rep. Henry Cuellar (D-Texas) said Tuesday that President-elect Trump’s tariffs “will definitely get Mexico to the table” to solve immigration and fentanyl problems.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani authorities start clearing Imran Khan supporters from the capital after deadly clashes
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistani security forces launched an operation Tuesday night to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who had gathered in the capital to demand his release from prison.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire after nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel approved a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah militants on Tuesday that would end nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the war in the Gaza Strip. The ceasefire, starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's House of Representatives passes bill that would ban young children from social media
item.description: Australia’s House of Representatives has passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon is set to begin at 4 am
item.description: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire with Hezbollah in Lebanon that will take effect at 4 a.m. Wednesday. Moments after U.S. President Joe Biden announced the ceasefire deal, which Israel's Cabinet approved late Tuesday, an Israeli airstrike slammed into the Lebanese capital.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: What both sides are saying about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group won backing from Israeli leaders.
item.dc:creator: Adam Geller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Unidentified drones spotted over US military sites in UK for second week
item.description: The Pentagon is “actively” watching for drones over U.S. Air Force locations in the United Kingdom, following a series of unexplained sightings over the past two weeks. The sightings, which took place over three U.S. air bases last week between Nov.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Biden to make new push for ceasefire in Gaza after Israel-Hezbollah deal
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday said the U.S. will make a new push for a ceasefire in Gaza in the coming days, after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to pause their fighting in and around the border with Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau says he had 'good call' with Trump amid tariff threats
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Tuesday he had a “good call” with President-elect Trump following the incoming U.S. leader’s pronouncement that he would impose a 25 percent tariff on Canadian goods when he takes office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: A fugitive wanted in the US for a pair of bombings is arrested in the UK after 20 years on the run
item.description: Authorities say a suspect wanted in the U.S. for two bombings in the San Francisco area in 2003 has appeared in a London courtroom after being captured in the U.K. The National Crime Agency says Daniel Andreas San Diego was arrested Monday in a rural area of Wales.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire to start Wednesday morning
item.description: The Israeli and U.S. governments announced a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon that will begin Wednesday morning at 4 a.m. local time. “I have good news from the Middle East,” President Biden said in remarks from the White House Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Germany’s Merkel: Trump ‘does not believe in any win-win situation,’ making collaboration difficult
item.description: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel argued President-elect Trump does not believe in “any win-win situation,” and it makes international collaboration difficult. Merkel, who served as chancellor from 2005-21, has seen U.S. presidents come and go.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Blinken to appear at Afghanistan withdrawal hearing, top Republican says
item.description: Rep. Michael McCaul (R-Texas), the outgoing chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Secretary of State Antony Blinken is expected to testify before the committee about the Biden administration's withdrawal of troops from Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Childhood cancer patients in Lebanon must battle disease while under fire
item.description: Lebanese families with children diagnosed with cancer are now grappling with the hardships of both illness and war.
item.dc:creator: Sally Abou Al Joud, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexican cattle imports suspended after New World screwworm detected
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) announced that a New World screwworm (NWS) was detected in Mexico on Friday, causing the agency to pause animal imports from the country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 6 dead as protests erupt in Pakistan over jailed former Prime Minister Imran Khan
item.description: Six people were killed as protests supporting imprisoned former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan escalated in Islamabad on Tuesday, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation was ordered detained on charges of sedition Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A violent showdown in Pakistan's capital pits the government against Imran Khan supporters
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Thousands of people are in the heart of Pakistan’s capital to demand the release of imprisoned former premier Imran Khan. He has been behind bars since August 2023 on charges that he and his supporters say are politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine president and vice president clash in a feud that's testing an Asian democracy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A major political storm set off by a bitter feud between the Philippine president and the vice president is testing an Asian democracy that has long been troubled by rowdy politics, poor law enforcement and bareface impunity.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian forces capture former British soldier fighting for Ukraine in Kursk: report
item.description: A former British Army soldier was reportedly captured by Russian forces while fighting alongside Ukrainian soldiers in the Kursk region of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Carrefour's cold shoulder for South American beef sparks a backlash from Brazil
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — Supermarket giant Carrefour’s support for French farmers’ protests against a trade agreement between the European Union and the South American bloc Mercosur has sparked a strong reaction in Brazil, including a refusal to supply beef to Carrefour stores in Brazil.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A far-right populist shocks Romania by heading into the presidential runoff
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania is reeling after a little-known, far-right populist secured the first round in the presidential election, beating the incumbent prime minister. Calin Georgescu, who ran independently, will face reformist Elena Lasconi in a Dec. 8.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scuffles in Serbian parliament as deadly station collapse sparks anger at the government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Scuffles and fistfights broke out between ruling party and opposition lawmakers in Serbia’s parliament on Monday, weeks after a deadly rail station roof collapse that ignited tensions in the Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine showdown: President says he'll fight vice president's plot to have him killed
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday described a public threat by the vice president to have him killed by an assassin as a criminal plot and vowed to fight it, in a looming showdown between the country’s two top leaders.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia's newly elected parliament opens its first session with opposition boycotting
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The newly elected Georgian parliament opened its inaugural session Monday as opposition lawmakers and the president boycotted it and thousands of protesters, watched by riot police, rallied outside and accused the ruling party of rigging the vote under Russian influence.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine to analyze fragments of missile fired by Russia capable of carrying nuclear warheads
item.description: Ukrainian investigators allowed journalists to view remains of an intermediate-range ballistic missile used by Russia on the battlefield, before an analysis could be completed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation
